groupSummary: Numerous claims highlight the links between ultra-processed foods and various health issues, including cancer, obesity, and diabetes.

claims.claimText: High UPF consumption is associated with increased risk of several cancers, particularly those affecting the digestive system and hormone-dependent organs.
claims.postExcerpt: Recent systematic reviews reveal a strong association between high UPF consumption and increased risk of several cancers.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are linked to 12 serious health conditions.
claims.postExcerpt: New research links ultra-processed foods to 12 serious health conditions.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods increase the risk of obesity, heart disease, diabetes, cancer, mental health disorders, and dementia.
claims.postExcerpt: These foods are quietly damaging your body, increasing your risk of obesity, heart disease, diabetes, cancer, mental health disorders, and dementia.

claims.claimText: Evidence shows that many preservatives in ultra-processed foods are significantly tied to increased cancer risk.
claims.postExcerpt: Data from @bmj_latest showed many preservative additives are significantly tied to increased risk for overall cancer development.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are linked to higher cancer risk, faster aging, and cognitive decline.
claims.postExcerpt: The hidden dangers of ultra-processed foods are now linked to higher cancer risk, faster aging, and cognitive decline.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods can harm our liver, gut, heart, brain, and hormones.
claims.postExcerpt: These foods are loaded with artificial colors, preservatives, excess sugar, unhealthy fats, and chemical additives.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are associated with chronic diseases and constant hunger.
claims.postExcerpt: Researchers are now linking ultra-processed foods to early death, chronic disease, and constant hunger.

groupTitle: Definitions and Characteristics of Ultra-Processed Foods
groupSummary: Several posts define ultra-processed foods and describe their characteristics, including ingredients and processing methods.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are significantly altered from their original form using industrial processes and ingredients not found in a home kitchen.
claims.postExcerpt: Ultra-processed foods are foods that have been significantly altered from their original form using industrial processes.

claims.claimText: Common ingredients in ultra-processed foods include artificial flavors, colors, preservatives, sweeteners, and emulsifiers.
claims.postExcerpt: They often contain: Artificial flavors, colors, or preservatives, sweeteners or emulsifiers.

claims.claimText: Examples of ultra-processed foods include packaged snacks, sugary drinks, and ready-to-eat meals.
claims.postExcerpt: Examples include: Packaged snacks (like chips or candy bars), sugary drinks, and ready-to-eat meals.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are often marketed as convenient but come with hidden health risks.
claims.postExcerpt: Ultra-processed foods: convenient, but at a cost.

claims.claimText: The NOVA classification system is used to categorize ultra-processed foods based on their level of processing.
claims.postExcerpt: According to the NOVA classification system—the gold standard used by researchers worldwide—ultra-processed foods are industrial creations.

groupSummary: Posts discuss the economic factors influencing the prevalence of ultra-processed foods and their impact on public health.

claims.claimText: The growing dependence on packaged and ultra-processed foods is driven by rapid lifestyle changes and marketing.
claims.postExcerpt: An MP highlighted the growing dependence on packaged and ultra-processed foods and warned about its long-term impact on public health.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are often more affordable and accessible than fresh, whole foods.
claims.postExcerpt: These foods are typically high in added sugar and are often more affordable than fresh produce.

claims.claimText: Aggressive marketing of ultra-processed foods contributes to chronic illness, especially among vulnerable populations.
claims.postExcerpt: This video covers: Ultra-processed foods and chronic disease, Factory farms.

claims.claimText: The economic model of food production favors ultra-processed foods over healthier options.
claims.postExcerpt: The dark side of modern food: How profits are harming public health.
